acceptance_targets: [CRAFT_STRUCTURAL_RECURRENCE_MIDDECK] Pass if, after Joel's clarifying line at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:243 and the laugh beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:249, the revised scene reaches a new irreversible split or decision by no later than the equivalent of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:291. Fail if the section between those beats and Paul's stand at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:323 still contains two separate argument cycles whose only new information is another restatement of how wrong Martin was.

[DIALOGUE_COMPOSED_DECK_DEBATE] Pass if the revised debate around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:205 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:299 contains at least one audible interruption or restart, at least one Nate line that overshoots or trails off instead of landing as a polished tag, and at least one Cliff/Tommy exchange where one man cuts the other off before he finishes a complete mini-speech. Fail if those stretches still read as alternating finished positions, quotable aphorisms, or rhetorical set-pieces.

[PROSE_CLOSE_THIRD_INTERIORITY_LAPSE] Pass if, between the equivalents of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:183 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:235, the revised scene contains at least two separate Joel-internal beats spaced through the argument so there is no run of 10 or more paragraphs without interiority, and if the replacements for snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:235 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:337 let the reader infer each man's stance from concrete behavior rather than abstract labels. Fail if the span still plays as long external dialogue patched afterward by explanatory summary.
[R13] Ch9 does not re-describe the Lagavulin scent as still present in the great room.
[length_chapter_09_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 4500 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after Joel's clarifying line at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:243 and the laugh beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:249, the revised scene reaches a new irreversible split or decision by no later than the equivalent of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:291. Fail if the section between those beats and Paul's stand at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:323 still contains two separate argument cycles whose only new information is another restatement of how wrong Martin was.
findings.problem: The chapter lands the hidden argument cleanly, but the middle of the deck scene reruns the same ethical debate in multiple cycles before it buys a new consequence. Nate's penalty-structure riff, the Kessler Rule riff, and the treason/tribunal exchange all keep litigating how bad Martin's offense was; only Paul's decision to tell Mallick changes the group's tactical state. That makes the chapter's center stretch feel like emphasis after arrival instead of fresh pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or restructure the repeat-debate stretches at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:223; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:253; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:291 so that one of those spans introduces the public consequence earlier. Preserve the fantasy-football ethics frame, Joel's brief cathartic laugh, and Paul's break at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:323, but cut at least one full cycle whose only work is reclassifying the affair's severity.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised debate around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:205 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:299 contains at least one audible interruption or restart, at least one Nate line that overshoots or trails off instead of landing as a polished tag, and at least one Cliff/Tommy exchange where one man cuts the other off before he finishes a complete mini-speech. Fail if those stretches still read as alternating finished positions, quotable aphorisms, or rhetorical set-pieces.
findings.problem: The post-reveal debate is funny, but too many turns arrive as finished comic briefs instead of pressured speech. Tommy gets a thesis-speech at the whole-rule line, Cliff gets aphoristic and quotable lines ('Not a burial plot,' 'sex legislation, scope matters'), and Cliff's tribunal sequence turns into a mini-speech diagnosis built around rhetorical questions. The scene stays readable, but it starts sounding written-about rather than overheard-through.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen the exchange at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:205; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:223; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:261; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:299 so the men interrupt, restart, overrun, or fail to land cleanly. Keep Tommy blunt and repetitive, Nate bit-driven, and Cliff smoother than the rest, but shorten the polished set-pieces and let at least one joke misfire or need rephrasing under scene pressure.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, between the equivalents of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:183 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:235, the revised scene contains at least two separate Joel-internal beats spaced through the argument so there is no run of 10 or more paragraphs without interiority, and if the replacements for snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:235 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:337 let the reader infer each man's stance from concrete behavior rather than abstract labels. Fail if the span still plays as long external dialogue patched afterward by explanatory summary.
findings.problem: The deck exchange runs for more than twenty consecutive paragraphs without Joel getting any live thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment, so the chapter temporarily reads like an external transcript. When interiority finally returns at line 235, it comes back as abstract diagnosis of what each man is doing rather than as Joel's immediate experience. The same thing happens again in the summary sentence at line 337. That combination weakens the style-bible requirement for close third colored by Joel's mind.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the deck section beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:183 and especially the return-to-interiority beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:235 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:337. Thread in brief Joel-specific internal beats during the argument itself, then replace the abstract taxonomy with embodied cues Joel would actually notice: what tightens, what loosens, where eyes go, what lands on him physically, what private memory or professional reflex the exchange triggers.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch9 does not re-describe the Lagavulin scent as still present in the great room.
findings.evidence: Ch4/916: 'The great room still smelled like Martin's scotch, the Lagavulin he'd poured too generously last night.' Ch7/1809: 'Martin's Lagavulin still hung in the great room, smoke and iodine caught in the paneling.' Ch9/2135: 'Martin's scotch, the Lagavulin, still sweetened the air in the great room beyond the kitchen.'
findings.problem: The Lagavulin-scent-in-the-great-room is re-described in nearly identical terms three times (Ch4, Ch7, Ch9). Ch9 is only two chapters after Ch7.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the Ch4 establishment. In Ch9/2135, cut the Lagavulin smell or replace it with a sensory detail showing time passing (the smell fading, the bottle depleting).

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 4500 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: The comedy is the creative risk and the creative achievement. These are men in their late forties who talk the way men in their late forties talk when there are no women present and the drinks have been flowing for hours: crude, raunchy, profane, sexually explicit, casually cruel, and occasionally accidentally beautiful. Do not clean this up. Do not soften the language. Do not make them witty where they would be vulgar. Do not make them eloquent where they would be clumsy. The profanity is not decoration — it is the texture of the friendship. When the second death breaks the comic register, the absence of that profane warmth should feel like a loss, not a relief. The murder, when it arrives, should be rendered with physical specificity — the water, the struggle, the sounds. The grief should be specific. The shame should be specific. The twenty-two-year-old guilt should be embodied, not summarized. If a scene makes the writer uncomfortable, that is a signal to stay in it longer, not to cut away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Every euphemism for death, sex, bodily function, emotional state, or financial desperation must be replaced with the direct term or a character-specific avoidance that itself reveals something about the character. Cliff saying 'restructure' when he means 'I'm broke' is character work. The narrator describing Cliff as 'facing financial challenges' is sanitization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the absolute norm. These are informal men in their late forties who have been friends for twenty-five years. Uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate emphasis, Joel's occasional legal precision, or Mallick's public-persona overcorrection. If two consecutive lines of dialogue contain uncontracted forms and neither character has a stated reason for formality, at least one is wrong.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. After a dialogue-heavy chapter, lead the next with interiority or description. After an interview chapter, give the reader a memory or a quiet observation. After a comic set piece, let the next chapter breathe. The scene counts vary (2-3) and should feel varied — a two-scene chapter after a three-scene chapter creates natural pacing shift. The tonal break at Chapter 14 should feel like a gear change, not a gradual dimming.
prose_style_profile.diction: Muscular, specific, unpretentious. No literary posturing. The prose should sound like intelligent conversation, not workshopped fiction. Prefer concrete nouns over abstract ones. Prefer active constructions. Avoid decorative metaphor — when a metaphor appears, it should be load-bearing. The comedy demands precision: funny is specific, and vague is never funny.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Prestige-literary inflation — do not elevate the comic register into literary solemnity. A man flushing Viagra while a detective knocks on the door is funny. The prose should be funny. Do not describe it as if it were a scene from Dostoevsky.

Comedic exhaustion — do not let every paragraph contain a joke. Comedy needs rhythm, which means it needs non-comic beats to land against. Let some paragraphs be straightforwardly descriptive or emotional.
Interior monologue sprawl — close third is not stream of consciousness. The POV character's interiority should be selective and purposeful, not an open channel of every thought.
Simultaneous narration — do not describe characters 'simultaneously' feeling multiple things. Choose the dominant feeling and let the undertone be implicit.
Forensic procedure porn — this is not a police procedural. Keep investigation details specific but brief. The comedy and character are the vehicle, not the forensics.

Metaphor creep — do not let the same metaphor template appear more than twice in a chapter. If you've used a sports metaphor, a water metaphor, and a fire metaphor, check that none of them are doing the same structural work.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV. Each chapter is locked to one character's interiority. No omniscient intrusions. The prose should take on the coloring of the POV character without becoming parody — Nate's chapters move faster and list more, Doug's chapters are quieter and more observational, Mallick's chapters shift between her two registers.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Conversational propulsion, not literary drift. The prose should move like someone telling you this story — not rushed, but never self-admiring. Vary sentence length aggressively: long compound sentences for the comic set pieces, short declarative hits for the turns. The humor lives in rhythm as much as content — the timing of a punchline is a prose-level decision.
sensory_bias: auditory — conversations, lake sounds, house creaks, the specific silence of a house where someone died
proprioceptive — hangovers, physical discomfort, adrenaline, the specific sensation of a body that hasn't slept
olfactory — booze, lake water, morning coffee, the basement must, the casserole
